assessment_items.question_content:
**Which technique did Titian use to paint the *Venus of Urbino*?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4a13c4b3abdfdad7ba681ed2da209f6a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Titian did not use tempera paint; he used glazes, or thin layers of translucent oil paint, to create this painting.**

choices.content: thin layers of tempera paint, called glazes
thin layers of oil paint, called glazes
thick layers of oil paint that create a three-dimensional surface

assessment_items.question_content:
The overt sensuality of this painting meant that its influence in art history was limited.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4a13c4b3abdfdad7ba681ed2da209f6a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This painting was enormously influential in art history. Nudes by Manet, Ingres, Picasso, and other artists were inspired by it.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of this painting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Titian was looking back to Giorgione's reclining nude (Sleeping Venus) for inspiration - not to Raphael's.**

choices.content: The painting is of a female nude and was given the title after it was painted, which justified the nude female figure.
With this painting, Titian established the genre of the reclining female nude that many later artists would use.
Titian manipulated the proportions of the body to make the figure especially elegant.
Titian was looking back to Raphael's nude for inspiration.
